evidence: plugin.json description names 5 skills; disk has 9 skill directories under skills/; nature-academic-search, nature-reader, nature-response, nature-writing absent from description text
description: plugin.json description enumerates only 5 of 9 shipped skills; 4 shipped skills are absent and 2 are erroneously listed as future releases
suggested_fix: Expand description text to include all 9 skills and remove the 'Future releases' claim for nature-response and nature-writing

evidence: No '## Output format' or '### Report results' section found in file; skill ends with Limitations section
description: Skill defines MCP tools and routing but does not specify what Claude should return to the user after completing a literature search workflow
suggested_fix: Add an '## Output format' section describing the default response structure (summary table, source list, dedup count, error notes)

evidence: No '## Output format' section found; skill ends with 'When NOT to load' and 'Related files' sections
description: Skill guides figure creation in detail but never specifies what Claude should return to the user (file paths, QA summary, code listing, or preview description)
suggested_fix: Add an '## Output format' section declaring the default deliverables: script path, export paths, and a short QA summary

evidence: 'Best For' column contains 'Broad academic search (scraped)' — 'broad' is an unconstrained quantifier with no operationalizable threshold
description: Two vague quantifiers: 'Broad academic search' and 'results may vary' give no actionable decision criteria
suggested_fix: Replace 'broad' with a concrete scope description (e.g., 'all disciplines, no API key required'); remove or qualify 'results may vary'

evidence: Lines 65, 89, 98 contain 'roughly 3000 characters', 'about 700 characters', 'about 10 segments'; line 254 contains 'usually 3-8 candidates'
description: Four vague approximation phrases that weaken decision rules: 'roughly', repeated 'about', and 'usually'
suggested_fix: Convert thresholds to exact integers or define tolerance bands (e.g., '> 3000 characters triggers batch mode'); replace 'usually' with a conditional rule

evidence: Frontmatter description line 4: 'high-impact journals'; When to load section: 'or similar venues'
description: Two vague scope terms: 'high-impact' has no operationalizable threshold; 'similar venues' provides no selection criteria
suggested_fix: Replace 'high-impact journals' with a named list or impact-factor floor; specify concrete examples after 'similar venues'

evidence: Line 193: 'usually select 4-8 figure/table assets'; lines 248-249: 'Speaker notes should be useful but concise'
description: Three vague quantifiers: 'usually', 'useful', and 'concise' leave selection and length criteria undefined
suggested_fix: Replace 'usually' with a conditional rule tied to slide count; define 'concise' in words or sentences (e.g., '2-4 sentences per slide')

evidence: Line 55: 'available on justified request'; line 47: 'suitable community repository exists'
description: Two vague qualifiers: 'justified request' and 'suitable community repository' lack operationalizable criteria
suggested_fix: Define what justifies a restricted-access request (legal, ethical, commercial); list criteria for 'suitable' repository (domain-indexed, DOI-minting, persistent)

evidence: Line 143: 'near the relevant discussion'; line 70: 'clearly lawful open-access content'
description: Two vague qualifiers: 'relevant' and 'clearly lawful' provide no operationalizable placement or legal criteria
suggested_fix: Define 'relevant discussion' as 'the paragraph that first mentions or interprets the figure'; replace 'clearly lawful' with a verifiable condition (e.g., CC-BY license or explicit open-access statement)

evidence: Line 29: 'Prefer concise, evidence-linked replies over long defensive explanations'
description: Two unanchored terms — 'concise' and 'long' — give no word-count or sentence-count bounds
suggested_fix: Define 'concise' and 'long' in explicit limits (e.g., 'concise: ≤ 150 words per point; long: > 300 words per point')

evidence: Line 297: 'clearly Chinese-influenced English' — 'clearly' sets no threshold for triggering this mode
description: 'Clearly Chinese-influenced' is an unanchored trigger; the condition for activating Chinese-to-English mode is ambiguous
suggested_fix: Replace 'clearly Chinese-influenced English' with explicit signals (e.g., 'contains Chinese characters, SOV sentence order, or direct character-by-character translation patterns')

evidence: Lines 19, 151, 153, 155: 'ambitious but bounded', 'concise notes', 'material issues', 'compact bullets'
description: Four vague quantifiers in the output format section leave deliverable quality undefined
suggested_fix: Replace 'ambitious' with a concrete scope qualifier; define 'concise', 'material', and 'compact' with word or item counts

evidence: ls skills/nature-reader/references/ shows grounding-rules.md and output-spec.md; grep of SKILL.md shows zero references to either filename
description: Two reference files exist in nature-reader/references/ but are never linked from SKILL.md; unclear whether they are drafts, superseded, or missing links
suggested_fix: Either add [link](references/grounding-rules.md) and [link](references/output-spec.md) in the appropriate SKILL.md sections, or delete the files if they are obsolete

description: PUBMED_EMAIL (from $1) is interpolated unquoted into a python3 -c heredoc; a crafted value like '); import os; os.system("cmd")#' executes arbitrary Python at install time
suggested_fix: Pass PUBMED_EMAIL as a Python argument or environment variable rather than interpolating it into source code: python3 -c 'import sys,json; ...' -- "$PUBMED_EMAIL"

evidence: Line 21: pip install --quiet mcp requests toml lxml — no version pins, no --require-hashes, installs to global or user site-packages
description: install.sh runs pip install with unpinned package names; a compromised or yanked-and-replaced PyPI package could be silently installed
suggested_fix: Replace with pip install -r requirements.txt using a fully pinned, hash-verified lockfile; or scope to a virtualenv

evidence: requirements.txt lines 1-4: mcp>=1.0.0, requests>=2.28.0, toml>=0.10.2, lxml>=4.9.0 — all use >= floor only
description: All four dependencies use >= without upper bound or hash; resolves to latest-available at install time, risking supply-chain drift
suggested_fix: Pin to exact versions with hashes via pip-compile --generate-hashes; add upper version bounds at minimum (e.g., mcp>=1.0.0,<2.0.0)
